label: Munich Agreement - Chamberlain appeases Hitler (September 1938)

decision_history.decision_taken: No military response; diplomatic protest only
decision_history.outcome: Germany emboldened

recent_events: Hitler threatening military action over Sudetenland

event.description: Hitler demands the Sudetenland region of Czechoslovakia be ceded to Germany, threatening military invasion if refused. Chamberlain flies to Munich to negotiate directly with Hitler, Mussolini, and Daladier — without Czechoslovakia present.

known_outcome.description: Chamberlain ceded the Sudetenland. Short-term de-escalation but emboldened Hitler.

label: Nazi-Soviet Non-Aggression Pact (August 1939)

core_narratives: Capitalist powers will destroy each other

decision_history.event_description: Failed negotiations with Britain and France
decision_history.decision_taken: Sought alternative security arrangement
decision_history.outcome: Western powers refused meaningful military commitment

ongoing_conflicts: Border war with Japan at Khalkhin Gol
recent_events: Western powers refusing Soviet alliance proposals

event.description: Germany proposes a non-aggression pact with secret protocols dividing Eastern Europe into spheres of influence. Stalin would gain eastern Poland, Baltic states, and Bessarabia. Germany gets free hand against western Poland.

known_outcome.description: Pact signed. Shocked the world. Enabled German invasion of Poland days later. Short-term de-escalation between Germany and USSR but enabled the wider war. Cynical realpolitik.

label: Britain declares war on Germany after Poland invasion (September 1939)

decision_history.decision_taken: Ceded Sudetenland to avoid war
decision_history.outcome: Hitler invaded rest of Czechoslovakia anyway

decision_history.decision_taken: Pledged to defend Polish independence
decision_history.outcome: Drew red line that Hitler crossed

event.description: Germany has invaded Poland in violation of the British guarantee. The British ultimatum demanding withdrawal has been ignored. Parliament and public demand action. Britain must decide whether to declare war.

known_outcome.description: Chamberlain declared war with heavy heart. 'Everything I have worked for has crashed into ruins.' Phoney War followed — escalation in commitment but not immediate combat.

label: Soviet invasion of Finland - Winter War (November 1939)

decision_history.decision_taken: Invaded Poland from east per Molotov-Ribbentrop secret protocol
decision_history.outcome: Absorbed eastern Poland with no Western response

recent_events: Western powers distracted by war with Germany

event.description: Soviet Union invades Finland with 600,000 troops after Finland refuses to cede territory near Leningrad. Stalin expects quick victory over small nation. Fabricates border incident as pretext.

known_outcome.description: Finland fought heroically, inflicting massive Soviet casualties. USSR expelled from League of Nations. Eventually won through attrition. Contained conflict — did not spread to wider war.

label: Fall of France - German invasion through Low Countries (May 1940)

decision_history.decision_taken: Ordered troops into demilitarized zone despite military advisors' objections
decision_history.outcome: No Allied response; emboldened further expansion

decision_history.decision_taken: Full-scale military invasion with Blitzkrieg tactics
decision_history.outcome: Poland conquered in weeks; triggered war declarations from UK and France

event.description: Germany invades France through the Low Countries (Belgium, Netherlands, Luxembourg), bypassing the Maginot Line with armored divisions. Blitzkrieg tactics overwhelm defenders, with Panzer divisions reaching the English Channel within days.

known_outcome.description: France fell within 6 weeks. Total war in Western Europe.

label: Hitler's Halt Order at Dunkirk (May 1940)

decision_history.decision_taken: Approved risky plan to attack through Ardennes
decision_history.outcome: Spectacular success; Allied armies split

recent_events: Göring claims Luftwaffe can finish them

event.description: German Panzer divisions have trapped 400,000 Allied troops at Dunkirk. Göring requests Luftwaffe handle destruction. Rundstedt advises halt to rest overextended tanks. Decision point: press ground attack or pause.

known_outcome.description: Hitler halted Panzers for 3 days. Allowed Dunkirk evacuation of 338,000 troops. Tactical pause within ongoing escalation.

label: Mussolini declares war on France and Britain (June 1940)

decision_history.decision_taken: Full-scale colonial war including poison gas
decision_history.outcome: Conquered Ethiopia; international condemnation

decision_history.decision_taken: Formal military alliance with Nazi Germany
decision_history.outcome: Bound Italy to German military adventures

military_posture.force_disposition: Military poorly equipped but large in numbers

event.description: France is collapsing under German assault. Mussolini sees opportunity to grab territory with minimal risk — 'a few thousand dead to sit at the peace table.' Italy declares war on France and Britain, attacking southern France.

known_outcome.description: Italy entered the war. Achieved little militarily against France. Opened Mediterranean theater. Eventually led to Italy's destruction.

label: Hitler orders bombing of British cities - The Blitz (September 1940)

decision_history.decision_taken: Blitzkrieg invasion
decision_history.outcome: Total success in 6 weeks

recent_events: RAF proving resilient in daylight battles

event.description: Hitler shifts Luftwaffe targeting from RAF airfields to London and British cities. Mass bombing of civilian areas begins — the Blitz. Goal is to terrorize the British population into demanding peace negotiations.

known_outcome.description: The Blitz killed 43,000 civilians but failed to break British morale. Actually relieved pressure on RAF airfields. Invasion postponed indefinitely. Escalatory in targeting civilians but strategically a mistake.

label: Roosevelt's Lend-Lease Act (March 1941)

decision_history.decision_taken: Signed neutrality legislation to keep US out of European wars
decision_history.outcome: Constrained US response options as war approached

decision_history.decision_taken: Modified neutrality to allow arms sales to belligerents who could pay cash and transport themselves
decision_history.outcome: De facto aid to Britain due to Royal Navy control of Atlantic

cultural_context.domestic_power_base: Democratic Party, labor unions, interventionist wing

recent_events: Britain running out of cash to buy arms
Battle of Britain won but UK battered

event.description: Britain is running out of dollars to purchase American arms. Churchill has written urgently requesting continued supply. Roosevelt proposes lending military equipment to nations whose defense is vital to US security — effectively ending neutrality without declaring war.

known_outcome.description: Lend-Lease passed. Made US the 'Arsenal of Democracy.' Escalatory step toward belligerency but not war itself. Germany viewed it as unneutral act.

label: Operation Barbarossa - German invasion of Soviet Union (June 1941)

decision_history.decision_taken: Blitzkrieg through Low Countries
decision_history.outcome: France fell in 6 weeks

historical_analogies: Napoleon's invasion of Russia (dismissed as irrelevant)

recent_events: Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact still nominally in force

event.description: Germany launches Operation Barbarossa with 3.8 million troops attacking the Soviet Union across a 2,900 km front, violating the non-aggression pact. Scorched earth tactics employed, mass executions of civilians and POWs from day one. Hitler orders war of annihilation.

known_outcome.description: Largest land invasion in history. War of annihilation on Eastern Front. Ultimately destroyed Nazi Germany.

label: Stalin's response to Operation Barbarossa (June 1941)

decision_history.decision_taken: Full-scale invasion over border dispute
decision_history.outcome: Pyrrhic victory; exposed military weakness

decision_history.decision_taken: Non-aggression pact with Nazi Germany, secret division of Eastern Europe
decision_history.outcome: Bought time but created false sense of security

military_posture.force_disposition: Officer corps decimated by purges, forces not on alert

recent_events: Multiple intelligence warnings of German attack dismissed by Stalin
German reconnaissance flights over Soviet territory increasing

event.description: 3.8 million German troops have crossed the Soviet border at dawn. Luftwaffe has destroyed much of the Soviet air force on the ground. Border armies are being encircled and destroyed. This is a war of annihilation targeting the Soviet state itself.

known_outcome.description: Stalin was paralyzed for days, then rallied. Total mobilization ordered. 'The enemy will be defeated. Victory will be ours.' Great Patriotic War began — existential struggle.

label: Roosevelt and Churchill sign Atlantic Charter (August 1941)

decision_history.decision_taken: Massive material aid to Britain
decision_history.outcome: US became Arsenal of Democracy, further from neutrality

event.description: Roosevelt and Churchill meet secretly at sea to issue a joint declaration of war aims and post-war vision — despite the US not being at war. Establishes shared principles: no territorial aggrandizement, self-determination, freedom of seas.

known_outcome.description: Diplomatic signaling. Made US-UK alignment public. Not a direct escalation but set post-war framework. Germany and Japan took note.

label: Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor (December 1941)

core_narratives: Liberation of Asia from Western colonialism

decision_history.decision_taken: Military occupation of Vichy French territory
decision_history.outcome: Triggered US oil and steel embargo

historical_analogies: Russo-Japanese War surprise attack on Port Arthur

event.description: Japan launches a surprise carrier-based air attack on the US Pacific Fleet at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii, destroying battleships and aircraft. Simultaneously attacks Philippines, Malaya, and other Pacific territories.

known_outcome.description: US declared war next day. Full Pacific War erupted. Unified American resolve and industrial mobilization.

label: Doolittle Raid on Tokyo (April 1942)

decision_history.decision_taken: Requested Congress declare war after Pearl Harbor
decision_history.outcome: Unanimous war declaration, national unity

military_posture.force_disposition: Building up but still on defensive in Pacific

event.description: US launches 16 B-25 bombers from carrier USS Hornet to bomb Tokyo and other Japanese cities. Militarily minor damage but first strike on Japanese homeland. Primarily a morale and propaganda operation.

known_outcome.description: Minor military impact but massive morale boost for US. Shocked Japan into Midway operation. Moderate escalation — war was already total but demonstrated US offensive reach.

label: Stalin's Order No. 227 - Not One Step Back (July 1942)

decision_history.decision_taken: No retreat orders, then strategic withdrawal
decision_history.outcome: Massive losses but bought time for Moscow defense

military_posture.force_disposition: Southern front collapsing, Germans driving toward Stalingrad and Caucasus oil

event.description: German forces are advancing rapidly toward Stalingrad and the Caucasus oil fields. The Southern front is collapsing. If Stalingrad falls, the Volga supply line is cut and Caucasus oil lost. The situation is desperate.

known_outcome.description: Stalin issued Order 227: 'Not one step back.' Blocking detachments authorized to shoot retreaters. Total commitment — Stalingrad became a death trap for both sides.

label: Operation Torch - Allied invasion of North Africa (November 1942)

decision_history.decision_taken: Germany First strategy agreed with Churchill
decision_history.outcome: Prioritized European theater over Pacific

military_posture.force_disposition: Large forces available, need combat experience

event.description: Allied forces land in French North Africa (Morocco, Algeria) with 107,000 troops. Facing Vichy French forces initially, with the goal of trapping Rommel's Afrika Korps between two Allied armies.

known_outcome.description: Vichy French resistance collapsed quickly. Allies secured North Africa by May 1943. First major US ground operation in European theater. Moderate escalation within existing total war.

label: Casablanca Declaration - Unconditional Surrender demand (January 1943)

decision_history.decision_taken: Joint war aims declaration with Churchill
decision_history.outcome: Established post-war vision

historical_analogies: Grant's unconditional surrender at Fort Donelson

recent_events: Need to reassure Stalin no separate peace

event.description: At the Casablanca Conference, Roosevelt announces that the Allies will accept nothing less than unconditional surrender from Germany, Italy, and Japan. No negotiations, no armistice — total defeat required.

known_outcome.description: Closed off all negotiated exits. May have stiffened Axis resistance. Reassured Stalin. Eliminated possibility of separate peace. Moderate-high escalation — locked in total war commitment.

label: Battle of Kursk - German last major offensive in East (July 1943)

decision_history.decision_taken: Refused to allow 6th Army to break out
decision_history.outcome: Entire army destroyed or captured. 300,000 casualties.

military_posture.force_disposition: Concentrated Panzer reserves for Kursk offensive

recent_events: Need a victory to restore initiative

event.description: Germany launches Operation Citadel — a massive pincer attack on the Kursk salient with 900,000 troops, 2,700 tanks, and 2,000 aircraft. Last attempt to regain strategic initiative on Eastern Front. Soviets know it's coming.

known_outcome.description: Largest tank battle in history. German offensive blunted within a week. Soviets counterattacked. Germany permanently lost initiative in the East. High intensity but within existing total war — escalation was continuing commitment, not new threshold.

label: D-Day - Allied invasion of Normandy (June 1944)

decision_history.decision_taken: Peripheral strategy attacking through Mediterranean
decision_history.outcome: Cleared North Africa, invaded Sicily and Italy

military_posture.force_disposition: Massive force assembled in southern England

event.description: Allies launch the largest amphibious invasion in history across the English Channel into Normandy. 156,000 troops, 5,000 ships, 11,000 aircraft. The liberation of Western Europe begins.

known_outcome.description: Successful landing. Opened Western Front Germany could not sustain. War already at maximum intensity — D-Day was decisive but within existing total war framework.

label: Allied firebombing of Dresden (February 1945)

decision_history.decision_taken: Approved Bomber Command targeting of German cities
decision_history.outcome: Massive destruction of German cities, civilian casualties
context_factors: Inability to hit precision targets at night

recent_events: Soviets requested bombing of Eastern German cities to aid advance
War clearly won but not over

event.description: RAF and USAAF launch massive firebombing raids on Dresden, creating a firestorm that destroys the city center and kills approximately 25,000 civilians. City is a cultural center with questionable military value at this stage of the war.

known_outcome.description: Massive destruction. Controversial even at the time — Churchill later distanced himself. Did not change war's outcome. Escalation in brutality within already-total war. Became symbol of strategic bombing's moral limits.

label: US drops atomic bomb on Hiroshima (August 1945)

decision_history.decision_taken: Demanded unconditional surrender with warning of 'prompt and utter destruction'
decision_history.outcome: Japan rejected the ultimatum
context_factors: Soviet entry into Pacific War imminent

military_posture.force_disposition: Preparing for invasion of Japan mainland

event.description: The United States drops an atomic bomb on Hiroshima, destroying the city and killing approximately 80,000 people instantly (140,000 by year end). First use of nuclear weapons in warfare. A second bomb is prepared for Nagasaki.

known_outcome.description: Unprecedented weapon. Japan surrendered after second bomb on Nagasaki and Soviet declaration of war. Maximum escalation — introduced nuclear age. The war ended but the world changed forever.

label: Japan's Supreme War Council debates surrender (August 1945)

decision_history.decision_taken: Endorsed military's decision for war
decision_history.outcome: Pearl Harbor and Pacific War

military_posture.force_disposition: Navy destroyed, air force reduced to kamikazes, army intact but isolated

ongoing_conflicts: Pacific War - losing on all fronts
recent_events: Soviet Union declared war and invaded Manchuria

event.description: Two atomic bombs have been dropped on Japanese cities. The Soviet Union has declared war and is sweeping through Manchuria. The Supreme War Council is deadlocked 3-3 on surrender. The Emperor must break the tie.

known_outcome.description: Hirohito broke precedent to order surrender: 'endure the unendurable.' De-escalation at maximum crisis point. Military coup attempt failed. Surrender broadcast August 15.
